dimensions.rationale: Receipt4 is hash-bound to the exact candidate/tree and declares LOCAL_SYNTHETIC_ONLY; inspected counters retain zero real Gmail, private-repository, protected, production, external-write and publication activity.

The receipt hash matches 3359865176269e7eb1da39aa38d6fc9e8230e530f8d02a25f3d8b5f4a3057fc2, binds the supplied baseline/candidate/tree, and records 15 distinct zero-exit local commands. The v2 validator requires five ordered attempts per family, ten distinct task IDs, exact artifact hashes, shared final subject/receipt/request, full finding closure, and protected post-review integrity; adversarial regressions cover the repaired transition and drift cases.

The deterministic status builder derives pre-closure unless assurance is PASS, only then derives the exact coupled v1.0.5 closed state, and closed validation additionally requires Git-bound integrity. The status schema rejects both crossed version/finding/blocker/next-action states; protected and production counters remain zero and production_health_claimed is false.

The candidate must descend from the frozen baseline and remain an ancestor of the delivery commit; later drift under all workflows and reviewed runtime, dependency, test, schema, status, package and assurance authority surfaces blocks closure. The reviewed evidence is local-only and does not create remote state requiring rollback.

findings.finding: PRODUCTION_RUN is capacity-gated and KILL-007 disables production execution, closing the prior UNKNOWN/RED capacity and kill-control bypass.
findings.resolution_ref: The preserved Terra history and current gate implementation apply fail-closed capacity and kill authorization before PRODUCTION_RUN.

findings.finding: Post-review integrity protects every workflow and the reviewed assurance/effect surfaces.
findings.resolution_ref: The protected set includes .github/workflows/ and the status, package, capture, validation, runtime, dependency, schema and test authority surfaces; adversarial Git cases reject protected drift.

findings.finding: The delivery-status transition no longer permits crossed pre-closure and closed facts.
findings.resolution_ref: Two full root alternatives bind v1.0.4 to the exact pre-closure state and v1.0.5 to the exact RMD-05-closed state; both crossed combinations have negative coverage.

findings.finding: The final v1.0.5 closure cannot be derived without the exact final two-family provenance chain or later protected-surface integrity.
findings.resolution_ref: Malformed, missing, stale, incomplete or non-PASS assurance selects only PRE_CLOSURE; only hash-checked final provenance selects CLOSED, and closed status validation requires clean Git-bound provenance.

limitations: This local read-only review does not prove protected Oracle execution.
It does not prove real Gmail or private-repository behavior, deployment, production health, production workflow execution, external writes, or publication.
It does not complete RMD-06, RMD-07, or final AC-033.
Repository artifacts prove structure, hashes and declared platform task identifiers only; platform audit-log retention remains required for independent platform-execution auditability.
summary: PASS: the exact candidate-bound local receipt and adversarially inspected RMD-05 controls resolve this reviewer’s history and explicitly resolve RMD05-CLOSURE-002 and RMD05-CLOSURE-003. The result is limited to assurance provenance and preserves all protected, production and publication blockers.
